revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:知立まつり]]
{{EventInformation |EventName=Chiryu Matsuri |EnglishName=Chiryu Matsuri |Type=[[Festival]] |image=Tiryuumatsuri7.JPG |imageSize=250px |Caption=Float and Tahoto Pagoda (Important Cultural Property) |CommonName= |OfficialName= |FormerName= |Timing=Annually on May 2 and 3 |FirstEvent=[[Edo period]] |Location=[[:en:Chiryu|Chiryu]], {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|qid=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}} |Organizer= |CoOrganizer= |Sponsor= |Collaborator= |Supporter= |Producer= |ExhibitorCount= |Visitors= |VenueAccessName= |NearestStation= |DirectBus= |Parking=None |URL=http://www.city.chiryu.aichi.jp/kanko_bunka_sports/kanko_bunka_sports/1/1451813709823.html |Notes= }} '''Chiryu Matsuri''' (知立まつり) is an annual festival held in May in [[:en:Chiryu|Chiryu]], {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|qid=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}}, associated with [[Chiryu Shrine]]. It has been celebrated since {{ill|Jōō (Edo period)|en|Jōō (Edo period)|qid=Q1145081|lt=Jōō|1=Jōō (Edo period)}} 2 (1653). The puppet theater performances "Yamabunraku" and "Karakuri," performed on floats during the main festival, are designated as {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|1=National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6573893}} of Japan. The festival is also part of UNESCO's {{ill|Yama, Hoko, Yatai Festivals|ja|山・鉾・屋台行事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yama, Hoko, Yatai Festivals|1=Yama, Hoko, Yatai, float festivals in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q28686576}} as an intangible cultural heritage. == Features == Centered around {{ill|Chiryu Shrine|ja|知立神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiryu Shrine|qq=}} and its surrounding area, the festival alternates annually between the Main Festival (''Hon Matsuri'') and the Intermediary Festival (''Ai Matsuri''). It is held over two days, May 2 and May 3. During the Main Festival, five towns (Nishimachi, Takaramachi, Yamamachi, Nakanishimachi, and Hommachi) present five floats, each measuring 7 meters tall and weighing 5 tons. On these floats, traditional puppet theater, known as ''Yamabunraku,'' and ''Karakuri'' puppet shows are performed.{{refnest|[https://www.chiryu-kanko.com/detail/view/22 Chiryu Matsuri Information] - Chiryu City Tourism Association|qq=}} During the Intermediary Festival, while ''Yamabunraku'' and ''Karakuri'' performances are not held, five ornate festival cars from the same towns are paraded. The Main Festival is held in even-numbered years. The ''Yamabunraku'' puppet theater and ''Karakuri'' puppet performances, dating back to the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, are recognized as {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties|1=National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6573893}} of Japan. Performances on floats are unique to Chiryu.{{refnest|[http://www.city.chiryu.aichi.jp/kanko_bunka_sports/kanko_bunka_sports/1/1451813709823.html Chiryu Matsuri] - Chiryu City Official Website|qq=}} The dual-layered performances, with ''Yamabunraku'' on the upper level and ''Karakuri'' on the lower level of the same float, are rare even within Japan. The ''Karakuri'' puppets, crafted entirely by local residents except for the heads, are made with unique techniques that use strings to animate the puppets. These techniques are preserved and passed down within each town. {{ill|Ryuhoku Junior High School|ja|知立市立竜北中学校|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryuhoku Junior High School|1=Ryuhoku Junior High School|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11584637}} also has a Bunraku Club that trains students in these skills. == History == According to the 1653 ''Chiryu Nakamachi Matsuricho'' (知立中町祭礼帳), the festival began during the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|qid=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, with four towns offering ''Karakuri'' performances to Chiryu Shrine. ''Bunraku'' performances on floats started in 1747. Over time, plays such as ''Sei Shōtoku Taishi Edenki,'' ''Yuruwaka Kōraigunki,'' ''Tekiuchi Ganryujima,'' and ''Ichinotani Gassen'' have been staged, reflecting the trends of their respective eras. In 1967, Chiryu's ''Karakuri'' was designated as a tangible folk cultural property of Aichi Prefecture.{{refnest|[https://www.pref.aichi.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazainavi/minzoku/yukeiminzoku/kensitei/0759.html Karakuri of Chiryu] - Aichi Cultural Property Navigator|qq=}} In 1979, it was selected as a national {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property in Need of Safeguarding|ja|選択無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property in Need of Safeguarding|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 記録作成等の措置を講ずべき無形の民俗文化財|1=Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11642584}}, and in 1990, "Chiryu’s Bunraku and Karakuri" was designated as a {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6573893}}. On December 1, 2016, it was registered as part of UNESCO's {{ill|Yama, Hoko, Yatai Festivals|ja|山・鉾・屋台行事|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yama, Hoko, Yatai Festivals|1=Yama, Hoko, Yatai, float festivals in Japan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q28686576}}.{{refnest|{{cite news|url=http://www.asahi.com/articles/ASJD10P5GJCZUCLV01B.html|title=Yama, Hoko, Yatai Festivals Registered as Intangible Cultural Heritage by UNESCO|newspaper=[[Asahi Shimbun]]|date=2016-12-01|qq=}}|qq=}} In 2020, due to the {{ill|COVID-19 pandemic|ja|新型コロナウイルス感染症の流行|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/COVID-19 pandemic|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 新型コロナウイルス感染症の世界的流行 (2019年-)|1=COVID-19 pandemic|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q81068910}}, the float parade was canceled. However, some rituals organized by Chiryu Shrine continued with reduced scale.{{refnest|''Chunichi Shimbun,'' April 1, 2020.|qq=}} In 2022, the float parade and performances were again canceled.{{refnest|{{Cite web|url=http://www.city.chiryu.aichi.jp/kanko_bunka_sports/kanko_bunka_sports/1/1451813709823.html|title=Chiryu Matsuri 2022: Notice of Parade Cancellation|publisher=Chiryu City|qq=}}|qq=}} == Gallery == {{Gallery |title= |footer= |width=200 |height=150 |lines= |File:Tiryuumatsuri1.JPG| |File:Tiryuumatsuri2.JPG| |File:Tiryuumatsuri4.JPG|Bunraku on a float |File:Tiryuumatsuri5.JPG|Karakuri performance |File:Tiryuumatsuri6.JPG| |File:Tiryuumatsuri3.JPG|Ruins of Chiryu Castle near Chiryu Shrine }} == Transportation == === By Train === * Approximately a 10-minute walk from {{ill|Chiryū Station|en|Chiryū Station|qid=Q5102035|lt=Chiryu Station|1=Chiryū Station}} on the {{ill|Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line|en|Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line|qid=Q1137838|lt=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line|1=Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line}}. === By Car === * About 5 km from the {{ill|Toyota-Minami Interchange|ja|豊田南インターチェンジ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyota-Minami Interchange|1=Toyota-minami interchange|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11634002}} on the {{ill|Isewangan Expressway|en|Isewangan Expressway|qid=Q6079783|lt=Isewangan Expressway|1=Isewangan Expressway}} via {{ill|Japan National Route 155|en|Japan National Route 155|qid=Q6156825|lt=National Route 155|1=Japan National Route 155}}. == References == {{Reflist|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/bsys/index National Designated Cultural Properties Database] * [http://www.city.chiryu.aichi.jp/kanko_bunka_sports/kanko_bunka_sports/1/1451813709823.html Chiryu Matsuri / Chiryu City] * [http://www.katch.ne.jp/~ksj/ Chiryu Karakuri Preservation Society] {{pref-stub-Aichi Prefecture|qq=}} {{Yama, Hoko, Yatai Festivals|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|知立まつり|version=98213573|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:知立まつり]]
<!--Chiryu festival--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{Commons category|Chiryu Matsuri}} {{wikidata link|Q11584632}} [[Category:Karakuri]] [[Category:山・鉾・屋台行事]] [[Category:Float festivals of Japan]] [[Category:愛知県にある国指定の重要無形民俗文化財]] [[Category:Festivals in Aichi Prefecture]] [[Category:愛知県指定民俗文化財]] [[Category:Bunraku]] [[Category:Chiryū, Aichi]] [[Category:記録作成等の措置を講ずべき無形の民俗文化財]]
